title: Cyrtochilum viminale
taxonomy: Cyrtochilum viminale is a species of orchid. It is classified in the family Orchidaceae. Cyrtochilum viminale is further classified in the subfamily Orchidoideae. The genus Cyrtochilum is a part of the subtribe Cynochiridinae. Cyrtochilum viminale is native to South America.

intro: Cyrtochilum viminale, also known as the "vine orchid," is a type of terrestrial orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyrtochilum viminale include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cyrtochilum viminale is often prepared as a tincture or tea, and is used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
